mermaid:
flowchart TB
    subgraph api_gateway_b[api-gateway — gateway]
    end
    subgraph catalog_service_b[catalog-service — api]
        catalog_service_products[("products")]
    end
    subgraph inventory_service_b[inventory-service — api]
        inventory_service_inventory_levels[("inventory_levels")]
        inventory_service_reservations[("reservations")]
    end
    subgraph notification_service_b[notification-service — worker]
    end
    subgraph order_service_b[order-service — api]
        order_service_orders[("orders")]
    end
    subgraph payment_service_b[payment-service — api]
        payment_service_payment_intents[("payment_intents")]
    end
    api_gateway_b -.-> order_service_b
    api_gateway_b -.-> catalog_service_b
    order_service_b -.-> inventory_service_b
    order_service_b -.-> payment_service_b
drawio_prompt:
Create a service_boundary diagram for the system "ecommerce-order-platform" targeted at the technical audience.

System description: Order capture, inventory reservation, payment, and notification on Azure microservices.

Components:
- api-gateway (gateway), depends on: order-service, catalog-service
- catalog-service (api), owns data: products
- inventory-service (api), owns data: inventory_levels, reservations
- notification-service (worker)
- order-service (api), depends on: inventory-service, payment-service, owns data: orders
- payment-service (api), owns data: payment_intents

Events:
- OrderCreated produced by order-service, consumed by: notification-service, inventory-service
- PaymentAuthorized produced by payment-service, consumed by: order-service

External systems:
- stripe (outbound)
- tax-jar (outbound)

Style: use C4-Model conventions where applicable. Keep components in a single boundary box for the system.
notes: Mermaid renders inline in GitHub-flavored markdown.
PlantUML can be rendered via any PlantUML host or extension.
The draw.io prompt is text — paste into draw.io's AI generator, or feed to a manual diagram tool.
